Title: The transcription factor ZmMYB69 represses lignin biosynthesis by activating ZmMYB31/42 expression in maize

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates ZmMYB31 and ZmMYB42; indirectly represses ZmCOMT1, Zm4CL3, and ZmCCR3
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Upper-level negative regulator of lignin biosynthesis in maize
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mechanical support; long-distance water transport; plant defense
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: secondary cell walls of vascular tissues and other highly lignified tissues
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression reduces lignin content and cell wall thickness and improves biomass saccharification; loss of function increases lignin content and cell wall thickness
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmMYB69 is mainly expressed in stem vascular tissues. 2. ZmMYB69 localizes to the nucleus and functions as a transcriptional activator. 3. Overexpression decreases lignin content and vascular cell wall thickness, whereas CRISPR/Cas9 mutants show increased lignin and thicker walls. 4. ZmMYB69 directly binds AC elements in ZmMYB31 and ZmMYB42 promoters and activates their expression. 5. ZmMYB69 indirectly suppresses lignin structural genes including ZmCOMT1, Zm4CL3, and ZmCCR3.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression in maize; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; lignin quantification; scanning electron microscopy; in situ PCR; subcellular localization; yeast transactivation assay; RT-qPCR; chromatin immunoprecipitation; EMSA; transient transcription activation assay in Nicotiana benthamiana; biomass saccharification assay
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Mainly expressed in stem vascular tissues; highly expressed in highly lignified tissues
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Mainly expressed in stem vascular tissues; highly expressed in highly lignified tissues
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Commercial cellulase treatment used to assess biomass conversion after altered lignin deposition
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression reduced plant height; loss-of-function mutants showed no visible growth defect reported
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to reduce lignin and improve biomass conversion/saccharification in maize biotechnology and breeding
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Ubiquitin promoter (constitutive) used for overexpression in maize
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although ZmMYB69 is a transcriptional activator, it represses lignin biosynthesis indirectly by activating the repressor TFs ZmMYB31 and ZmMYB42.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional repression of lignin biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses expression of lignin biosynthetic genes including ZmCOMT1, Zm4CL3, and ZmCCR3
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Known repressor of lignin biosynthetic genes acting downstream of ZmMYB69
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mechanical support; long-distance water transport; plant defense
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: secondary cell walls of vascular tissues and other lignified tissues
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Its upregulation is associated with reduced lignin biosynthesis downstream of ZmMYB69
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmMYB31 expression is increased in ZmMYB69 overexpression lines and reduced in ZmMYB69 CRISPR mutants. 2. ZmMYB69 directly binds the ZmMYB31 promoter at AC elements and activates its expression. 3. ZmMYB31 is a known repressor of lignin biosynthetic genes in maize.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; ChIP; EMSA; transient transcription activation assay; inference from ZmMYB69 perturbation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in ZmMYB69 overexpression stems and downregulated in ZmMYB69 CRISPR mutant stems
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential downstream target for lignin reduction engineering
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: ZmMYB31 native promoter contains AC elements targeted by ZmMYB69

ZmMYB69 directly binds the ZmMYB42 promoter at AC elements and activates its expression. 3. ZmMYB42 is a known repressor of lignin biosynthetic genes in maize.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; ChIP; EMSA; transient transcription activation assay; inference from ZmMYB69 perturbation
